title: 10 best herbal creams for jaw lock

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock, making it easier to relax the muscles and alleviate discomfort.
Muscle-relaxing properties: The soothing and calming effects of chamomile help to relax tense jaw muscles, reducing tension and spasms that can contribute to jaw locking.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties help to calm the mind and body, which can alleviate stress-related jaw locking caused by clenching or grinding teeth due to anxiety.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with jaw lock because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm muscle tension and spasms. The herbal extract in chamomile creams relaxes the jaw muscles, reducing pain and stiffness associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation, chamomile creams can help alleviate jaw lock symptoms, improving overall oral health and reducing discomfort. Regular use can lead to improved jaw mobility and reduced stress.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
Chamazulene: An isobutylbenzene derivative that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relax muscles and reduce spasms in the jaw.
Bornyl acetate: A terpene that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Matricaria chamomilla in creams for jaw lock due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used in creams for jaw lock due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which helps to ease jaw tension.
Leaves: The leaves are used in creams for jaw lock due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for the herbal infusion base.
Steep the dried flowers in 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids.
Mix 25 grams of emulsifying wax and 25 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Blend the cooled herbal infusion with the melted wax mixture and 100 milliliters of distilled water.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream helps to relax muscle spasms in the jaw and surrounding areas, reducing tension and alleviating pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lemon balm's ability to calm the nervous system helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers of jaw lock and tension headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with jaw lock because of its calming and relaxing properties. The herb's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects help to ease muscle tension and reduce pain associated with jaw lock. By soothing the jaw and facial muscles, Melissa officinalis creams can help to alleviate the discomfort and stiffness often experienced during an episode of jaw lock. Regular use of these creams may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, contributing to overall jaw health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound found in Melissa officinalis, which may help with jaw lock by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the face and jaw.
Linalool: A terpene with sedative and anxiolytic properties, which could help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw lock.
Melissic acid: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the jaw and facial muscles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of compounds like borneol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw pain.
Stems: Utilized for their rich content of antioxidants and essential oils, which can provide soothing and relaxing effects on the jaw area.
Roots: Employed for their bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw joint.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100 grams of dried melissa officinalis flowers in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:10.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the bottle every day or two.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter with 100 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for about an hour or until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian root's sedative and muscle-relaxing compounds help to relax the muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, which can become tense and locked due to stress, anxiety, or other factors.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in valerian creams can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, a common cause of jaw lock and discomfort.
Sedative and Anxiolytic Properties: Valerian's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related tension that may contribute to jaw lock, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with jaw lock because of its natural muscle-relaxing properties. The root extract of valerian has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm and relax the jaw muscles, reducing tension and spasms that can lead to jaw lock. Additionally, valerian's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the jaw area, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort associated with jaw lock. This can lead to improved sleep and reduced stress levels.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has muscle-relaxing properties that help alleviate jaw tension and spasms associated with jaw lock.
Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a branched-chain fatty acid, has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce anxiety and stress-related jaw lock.
Valeranone: Valeranone, a sesquiterpene, has a calming effect on the nervous system and may help reduce muscle spasms, making it beneficial for treating jaw lock.

Barks: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the facial area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 valeriana officinalis roots in the fall when the plant is mature to ensure maximum potency.
Clean the valeriana officinalis roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush under cold running water.
Steep 1 cup of valeriana officinalis roots in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Passionflower: Passionflower has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which can contribute to jaw clenching and locking due to tension and stress relief. Its sedative effects help calm the nervous system and relax the muscles.

By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, these creams can help to alleviate jaw lock, allowing for a more comfortable and peaceful state. remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a beta-carboline alkaloid that has anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing tension and alleviating jaw lock symptoms.

Methanol extract's flavonoids and phenolic acids: The methanol extract of Passiflora incarnata contains various flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle tension and inflammation in the jaw, thereby alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to relieve jaw lock pain.
Roots: Contain passifloric acid and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease jaw tension.

remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers at dawn when moisture levels are high for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested passiflora incarnata in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Steep 20 grams of dried passiflora incarnata in 1000 ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter the oil through a coffee filter.
Mix 10% of the passiflora incarnata infused oil with 90% of a water and glycerin mixture to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw locking (TMJ disorder).
Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress and promote healing in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) area.
Tannins: The tannins present in St. John's Wort creams help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can aid in reducing inflammation and swelling in the jaw joint.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, creams helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been found to reduce pain and swelling in the muscles and joints. When applied topically in the form of a cream, these compounds can help relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension, making it easier to move the jaw and alleviate the pain associated with jaw lock. remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.
Stems: The stems are used for their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties, which make them effective in treating jaw lock and related muscle tension.
Roots: The roots are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a trusted source at dawn.
Weigh 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in separate containers for blending.
Combine 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of jojoba oil in a saucepan and heat.
Add 100 grams of the hypericum mixture to the saucepan and stir for 20 minutes at low heat.
Pour the mixture into a 200-gram glass jar and let it cool and harden for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Wormwood creams contain compounds that relax muscle spasms, helping to reduce tension and alleviate jaw locking (TMJ) symptoms by calming the muscles responsible for tooth clenching and grinding.

Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate is a monoterpene that has a sedative effect and can help relax the muscles, which may help relieve jaw lock caused by stress or anxiety.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers to ensure high quality and potency of the cream.
Infuse 200g of leaves and flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal extract.
Mix 100g of the herbal extract with 200g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir continuously until the beeswax has melted and the cream has thickened.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers at the peak of bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the fresh flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 6 hours.
Infuse 100g of the dried flowers in 500ml of jojoba oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and add the infused oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_constituents: Citral: Citral is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles, thereby alleviating jaw lock.
Geraniol: Geraniol is a terpene that has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing spasms and tension that may be contributing to jaw lock.

remedy_recipe: Extract 250ml of cymbopogon citratus essential oil using a steam distillation process for 3 hours.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter in a double boiler at 60°c for 10 minutes.
Combine 5ml of cymbopogon citratus essential oil with 10g of vitamin e oil in a small bowl.
Add the essential oil mixture to the beeswax mixture and blend using a hand mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and joints, easing tension and discomfort associated with jaw locks.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of aloe vera helps neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the jaw area, contributing to conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

This helps to relax the jaw muscles, relieving tension and stiffness that can cause jaw lock. remedy_constituents: Bradykininase: Bradykininase, an enzyme found in Aloe vera, helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can contribute to jaw lock by easing muscle tension in the jaw area.
Glucomannan: Glucomannan, a polysaccharide present in Aloe vera, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, thereby alleviating jaw lock.

Aldehydes (including cinnamaldehyde and vanillin): Aldehydes, such as cinnamaldehyde and vanillin, found in Aloe vera, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw, making it easier to relax and release jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which is often used in creams due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from the Leaves: The clear, jelly-like substance is extracted from the leaves and used in creams to reduce inflammation and relax jaw muscles.
Aloe vera Extract from Leaves: It is often used in creams due to its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and swelling associated with jaw lock and other conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g aloe vera gel extract and 50g beeswax in a stainless steel bowl.
Melt 50g coconut oil and 25g shea butter in a double boiler at 180f for 10 minutes.
Mix 25g vitamin e oil and 10g essential oil with the melted coconut oil mixture.
Combine the aloe vera gel extract mixture with the melted oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula, the main active compound in pot marigold, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw locks.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency and minimal moisture content.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the oil.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jaw lock because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that target the source of tension in the jaw. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered taraxacum officinale with 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before mixing with 50g of beeswax.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool to solidify and thicken the cream.

Valerian: Valerian root has been traditionally used for its sedative and relaxing properties, which may help to calm tense muscles and improve sleep quality, potentially reducing jaw lock symptoms caused by stress or anxiety.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir until melted and combined.
Remove from heat and let cool for 15 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars and store in refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baby rubber plant creams helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to relax and soothe the muscles in the face and jaw. The calming properties of these herbs help to reduce tension and inflammation, allowing the jaw joint to function properly and alleviating symptoms of TMJ disorders such as locking or clicking. Regular use of these creams can provide long-lasting relief from jaw lock and promote overall oral health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): May help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints, alleviating symptoms of TMD.

Lavender: Known for its calming and relaxing properties, lavender oil or extract might help reduce stress and tension in the jaw muscles, contributing to a lockjaw-like sensation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of alkaloids that help relax tense muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried lavender buds.
Combine the dried herbs with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler setup on medium heat.
Stir gently for 10 minutes or until the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and mix until melted, then pour into jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with jaw lock because of its cooling and relaxing properties. When applied topically, the menthol in peppermint oil can help ease tension and spasms in the jaw muscles, providing immediate relief from locking or grinding sensations. As the cream soothes and calms the affected area, it also reduces inflammation and promotes blood flow, allowing for a more natural alignment of the teeth and jawbone, thus resolving the lock. Regular use of herbal peppermint creams can lead to a significant reduction in frequency and severity of jaw locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and surrounding muscles, providing relief from tension headaches and jaw lock.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of peppermint can numb the pain associated with jaw lock, allowing for temporary relief from discomfort and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds, such as borneol, can help relax tense muscles in the jaw, face, and neck, which can contribute to jaw lock and related issues like migraines and TMJ disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing jaw lock pain.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of peppermint plants contain a high concentration of menthol, which provides immediate relief from jaw tension and muscle spasms associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried peppermint leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground peppermint powder with 8 ounces of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over medium heat.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to ensure the infusion process is complete.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Whip 8 ounces of coconut cream with the infused peppermint oil and add 2 drops of lavender essential oil for an additional 3 minutes until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique combination of natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds. The cream's active ingredients, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work together to reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles, alleviating the discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower creams, such as polyphenols and terpenes, can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of chronic jaw tension and lockjaw.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers at the rate of 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of cream base.
Combine the harvested flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids; set aside for later use.
Mix the strained oil with 8 ounces of unscented cream base and 2 teaspoons of beeswax, heating until melted and blended in a double boiler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with jaw lock because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that soothes and calms inflamed tissues. When applied topically to the affected area, it reduces swelling and relaxes tense muscles, thus alleviating tension and discomfort caused by TMJ disorder. Additionally, milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and stiffness in the jaw joint, allowing for smoother movement and relief from lockjaw symptoms.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by inflammation, promoting overall health and reducing symptoms of jaw lock.

Silymarin: Silymarin, a bioflavonoid found in milk thistle, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can specifically target and reduce pain and stiffness associated with jaw locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain silymarin, which helps reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension in the jaw, alleviating lockjaw symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried silybum marianum leaves and stems from a trusted source or storehouse.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered silybum marianum with 30 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before transferring it to sterilized glass jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium creams helps with jaw lock because of its unique ability to calm and soothe the muscles in the face and neck. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce tension and swelling, allowing for a more relaxed state of the jaw and surrounding areas. By applying a cream containing wild geranium extract, individuals can experience relief from tension headaches, migraines, and TMJ disorders, promoting overall facial relaxation and well-being.

Antispasmodic: The essential oils present in wild geranium, such as geraniol and linalool, exhibit antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscle tension and reduce spasms in the jaw muscles that contribute to jaw lock.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried geranium maculatum leaves and stems from local organic farms or your own garden.
Combine the dried herbs with 1/4 cup of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan, placing it on low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets with 1/8 cup of the infused oil in a small bowl, heating gently until melted and smooth.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer to create a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with jaw lock because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and relax the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw. Antispasmodic: Angelica's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing tension and spasms that can cause jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax tense muscles in the jaw.
Barks: Contain volatile oils with analgesic and antiseptic properties, reducing pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, providing nourishment to the skin and promoting healthy tissue growth for a relaxed jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried angelica archangelica root and 1 ounce of beeswax pellets.
Combine the dried angelica archangelica root with 4 ounces of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the herbs release their properties into the oil.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of jojoba oil to the cooled herbal infusion and whip until emulsified.
Mix the whipped cream base with 8 ounces of coconut milk and 2 ounces of shea butter, then pour into sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with jaw lock because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. The calming properties of the herbs, such as chamomile and lavender, help to soothe the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding muscles, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with jaw locking. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and leaves for use in this recipe.
Infuse dried rosa rugosa petals in carrier oil for 4 weeks at room temperature.
Combine infused oil with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 160°f while stirring occasionally for 30 minutes then cool.
Whip the cooled mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with jaw lock because its calming properties soothe tension in the muscles of the face, particularly the masseter muscle responsible for jaw clenching. The cream's anti-inflammatory and relaxing effects ease stiffness and discomfort, allowing the jaw to release its tight grip. As a result, the cream provides quick relief from stress-induced jaw locking, promoting relaxation and reducing the risk of chronic pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which often cause jaw locking.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of English lavender help soothe and relax the jaw muscles, reducing tension and pain associated with jaw lock.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, the barks of English lavender have anti-inflammatory properties that ease jaw discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of cream.
Combine 200g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 400ml of high-proof vodka in a dark glass jar and let it infuse for 6 hours.
Strain the infused vodka mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 20ml of the infused vodka extract in a double boiler and heat until fully incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool to 60°c before transferring the mixture into glass jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaw lock are topical preparations containing plant extracts that help alleviate tension and pain in the jaw area, often caused by stress, anxiety, or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. The best herbal creams for jaw lock include Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Gaultheria procumbens, Lavandula angustifolia, Calendula officinalis, Arnica montana, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, jaw lock can lead to headaches, difficulty eating, and sleep disturbances, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw lock that are treated with herbal creams are various musculoskeletal issues, such as tennis elbow, carpal tunnel syndrome, and fibromyalgia. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, St. John's Wort, and capsaicin help alleviate pain and inflammation in these conditions by reducing muscle tension, improving circulation, and stimulating the release of natural pain-relievers in the body. These topical treatments offer a safe and effective alternative to oral medications and surgery.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with jaw lock is a blend of arnica, chamomile, and peppermint. Arnica helps to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the jaw, while chamomile calms and soothes the muscles, promoting relaxation. Peppermint, with its cooling properties, provides instant relief from pain and discomfort. When applied together, these creams create a powerful synergy that helps to loosen and release the jaw, providing long-lasting relief from jaw lock and promoting overall well-being. Regular use can also help to prevent future occurrences.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with jaw lock because they contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that reduce muscle tension and pain in the jaw area. The menthol and eucalyptus oil in these creams have a cooling effect, which helps to relax the muscles and relieve the tension that causes jaw locking. By reducing inflammation and soothing the jaw muscles, Eucalyptus globulus creams provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with jaw locking, promoting relaxation and a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw area, alleviating the symptoms of jaw lock.
Limonene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain associated with jaw lock, allowing for more comfortable jaw movement.
α-Pinene: It acts as a bronchodilator and can help relax the muscles in the jaw, throat, and face, thereby reducing tension and alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential Oil - The leaves are used for their essential oil, which is the primary component extracted for various applications, including creams for jaw lock relief.
Barks: Tannins and Phenolics - The bark contains tannins and phenolics, which are often used in creams for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: Glycosides - The leaves also contain glycosides, which are used in creams to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from jaw lock pain.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of eucalyptus globulus leaves in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Combine 200ml of distilled water with the extracted eucalyptus globulus in a heat-proof bowl.
Add 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil to the heat-proof bowl.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 30 minutes until it reaches 60c.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and refrigerate for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with jaw lock because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds, such as menthol and menthone, in peppermint oil work to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension. When applied topically, these compounds penetrate deep into the skin and muscle tissue, providing quick relief from pain and stiffness. By relaxing the jaw muscles, Mentha x piperita creams can help alleviate jaw lock, also known as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, and promote overall oral health and well-being.

Menthone: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pain and swelling in the jaw, and relieving tension that may lead to jaw lock.
Rosenusene: Acts as a natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relax the muscles in the jaw and alleviate spasms, thereby reducing the severity of jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides a cooling sensation and helps relieve jaw pain and tension.
Stems: Stems contain menthol and other compounds that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in creams for jaw lock.
Roots: Roots of Mentha x piperita are also used in creams for jaw lock due to their rich content of menthol and other bioactive compounds that help soothe and relax the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes to create a strong herbal extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean container to remove the solids.
Combine 100g of beeswax, 100g of coconut oil, and 20g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl to prepare the cream base.
Heat the cream base mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes to melt the ingredients.
Add 10ml of the herbal extract and 2g of vitamin e oil to the cooled cream base mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream contains tea tree oil, which has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw. This is particularly beneficial for individuals who experience jaw lock due to conditions such as TMJ disorder or bruxism. The cream's soothing properties can help relax the jaw muscles, reducing tension and promoting relief from discomfort. Regular use may lead to long-term relief from jaw lock.

Stems: The stems are sometimes used to extract smaller amounts of tea tree oil, which can be added to creams to provide additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers in a clean and sterile environment.
Infuse the harvested plant material in 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-3 weeks or until it reaches a desired colour.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and a preservative in a heat-proof container and heat until it melts.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with jaw lock because they contain ginger's anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and swelling in the jaw area. The warming sensation of the cream also increases blood flow, promoting relaxation and easing tension in the jaw muscles. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic properties numb the pain, providing temporary relief from the discomfort associated with jaw lock, making it a popular natural remedy for this common condition. This helps to restore normal jaw function.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help with jaw lock by reducing inflammation and pain through their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaols: Assist in alleviating jaw lock by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress.
Zingerone: Contributes to the relief of jaw lock by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help in reducing muscle spasms and pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the ginger plant, used due to its high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

(Optional) Leaves: Although less commonly used, ginger leaves might be used in some cases, but their primary use is in traditional medicine for other purposes.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 tablespoon of zingiber officinale root powder from 30 grams of dried root in 10 minutes.
Melt 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at 150-180 degrees fahrenheit in 5 minutes.
Combine 1 tablespoon of zingiber officinale root powder with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in 2 minutes.
Stir the mixture of zingiber officinale root powder and beeswax into the melted coconut oil in 5 minutes.
Pour the zingiber officinale mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with jaw lock because they provide soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that ease tension and pain in the jaw muscles. The gel extracted from Aloe vera contains compounds that relax and calm the muscles, reducing spasms and stiffness. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Aloe barbadensis creams can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with jaw lock, promoting overall well-being and jaw health. Glucomannans: Act as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the jaw joint, and helping to ease jaw lock.
Saponins: Have anti-spasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, making it easier to move and alleviating jaw lock.

remedy_recipe: Measure 50g of pure aloe barbadensis gel extracted from the plant's inner leaf into a clean glass bowl.
Weigh 100g of distilled water and heat it to 70-80°c for 5-7 minutes in a double boiler.
Mix 10g of vegetable glycerin with the warm water mixture to enhance the cream's texture and stability.
Combine 50g of sweet almond oil with 10g of beeswax and 5g of candelilla wax in a separate saucepan.
Blend the aloe barbadensis gel mixture with the oil and wax mixture using a handheld electric mixer until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens creams helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb, also known as wintergreen, contains methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. When applied topically, it can help reduce muscle tension and inflammation in the jaw area, easing the pain and stiffness associated with jaw lock. The cream can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, contributing to a more comfortable and relaxed jaw and facial muscles. This natural remedy can provide relief without the need for pharmaceuticals.

Gaultherin: Helps with jaw lock by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint disorders.
Volatile oils (specifically, bornyl acetate and borneol): Helps with jaw lock by providing a localized anesthetic effect, numbing the pain and discomfort in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Steep 2 teaspoons of the dried gaultheria procumbens in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a glass bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of the herbal liquid to 4 ounces of distilled water and mix into the cooled beeswax-coconut oil mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with jaw lock because of its natural anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a clean and well-maintained garden or purchase from a reputable supplier.
Infuse 50g of the dried flowers in 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 100g of shea butter and 50g of beeswax with the infused oil in a double boiler.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before pouring into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Calendula creams can provide quick relief from jaw tension, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable movement of the jaw.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid in Calendula officinalis, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation in the jaw, thus contributing to the relief of jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and calm jaw pain.
Leaves: They are often used due to their astringent properties, which help reduce swelling and ease jaw tension.
Seeds: They are sometimes used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate jaw pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for 2 to 3 days or until brittle.
Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the oil for melting.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a dedicated soap making pot at a temperature of 120 to 140 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before whipping it into a smooth and creamy texture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the jaw area. The cream's soothing action calms tense muscles, easing the discomfort associated with jaw locking. Additionally, Arnica's ability to improve blood flow and reduce stiffness helps to relax the jaw muscles, making it easier to move the jaw without pain. This natural remedy provides relief from jaw tension and promotes relaxation in the face and neck.
remedy_constituents: Thiophene derivatives: Help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the jaw, thereby alleviating jaw lock and pain.
Alkamides: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the jaw muscles, reducing tension and pain associated with jaw lock.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the jaw area, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw lock.
Leaves: Leaves of Arnica montana are also used to create creams for jaw lock, but the specific reasons aren't as clear, and further research is needed.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried arnica montana flowers and add them to a clean glass jar.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the double boiler.
Add 100 ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool for at least 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids have analgesic effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, Salvia officinalis creams may promote relaxation and reduce muscle spasms, allowing the jaw to release its locked position and return to a normal functioning state. remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: It helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, which can contribute to jaw lock.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve muscle spasms and tension in the jaw.

remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves from a well-maintained garden or a trusted supplier when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 200 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 500 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a glass jar for storage and use as needed.
